human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for payments-operations specificity, posting and reconciliation evidence quality, approval-control discipline, fraud and AML escalation posture, consumer-protection handling, and validated completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to payment intake, posting readiness, remittance validation, denial and return handling, reconciliation, and exception escalation rather than accounting signoff, underwriting, or fraud adjudication.

Authority sources cover Federal Reserve ACH and wire workflow rules, CFPB consumer-protection rules, FFIEC recordkeeping and suspicious-activity boundaries, and FDIC plus CPMI-IOSCO control standards.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal payment handling, missing evidence, unsupported overrides, reconciliation mismatches, fraud and consumer-impact escalation, policy conflicts, low-confidence handling, adjacent-lane rejection, and tenant-specific adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/financial-services/payments-operations-specialist/ so payments operations work is distinct from adjacent fraud, AML, legal, underwriting, servicing, and accounting lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around unsupported overrides, accounting-treatment requests, suspicious activity, consumer-impact handling, and source-conflict escalation.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated payments operations specialist for evidence-backed posting, reconciliation, and financial exception handling.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
